session_note: S54 — autonomous batch pre-approved by user ('work top recommended priority batch autonomously i pre-approve your full batch and work. be thorough pedantic methodical creative and technical'). Standard-tier 2-priority batch activating the dormant S53 P1 protocol-mismatch system. P1 — HELLO emission at peer-join (wire the missing producer: buildHello() in protocol.ts + shared wireHelloOnJoin helper registered via transport.onPeerChange('join') on BOTH host (playerId 0 / crimson) and joiner (playerId 1 / cyan); extract testable handleRawMessage seam in transport.ts + integration tests proving the latch fires/drops — Grok R8 HIGH + Gemini ch.3 CONVERGENT). P2 — CHECK-CARRY-S54 LOW cluster (G3 sanitize verbatim peerVersion; M4 type-guard replacing the 'as Record' cast in detectProtocolMismatch; M5 neutral 'The other player's version is older' UX text + update transport.test.ts:167 assertion). A.0 STATE-DISCOVERY: grep-verified NO production send({kind:'HELLO'}) exists; onPeerChange confirmed unused API (main.ts polls peerCount()); transport.test.ts confirmed to lack onMessage-path integration coverage. 3-way Council R1 (Grok DISRUPTOR temp 0.4 + Gemini AUDITOR temp 0.5). PRIME-AUDIT delta: activation arms FUTURE protocol-skew detection ONLY (pre-S54 peers emit no HELLO) — zero observable behavior today beyond happy-path no-op; forward-looking insurance completing the S53 half-build.
council_battle_ledger: 7-row ledger. (1) Emission point onPeerChange('join') — Grok+Gemini CHALLENGE (race/ordering) -> SYNTHESIS: keep hook, rely on lobby-phase timing guarantee (no snapshots/intents flow pre-Begin-Match so HELLO is provably first) + doc comment, no over-engineered sendQueue. (2) Both-sides-emit vs host-only — Grok CHALLENGE (host-only) -> OVERRULED: host-side latch REQUIRES the joiner's HELLO to close the v2-bypass desync gap (S53 CONVERGENT BLOCKER artifact); Grok's 'host rejects on receipt' false for semantically-valid INTENTs from a skewed peer. (3) M5 neutral text — Grok+Gemini AGREE -> AGREED (unanimous) ship. (4) buildHello location — Grok CHALLENGE -> SYNTHESIS: buildHello in protocol.ts + tiny shared wireHelloOnJoin helper for DRY without coupling transport to game-role. (5) Same-version HELLO wire hazard — AGREED none (handlers ignore kind:'HELLO'). (6) Mismatch-path integration test — Grok R8 HIGH + Gemini ch.3 -> ADOPTED: extract handleRawMessage(data,peerId) seam + test mismatched-HELLO fires callback + latches + drops subsequent. (7) Targeted send vs broadcast — Gemini ADD -> PARTIAL/DEFER: broadcast==targeted for 1v1, comment + Phase-3 carry. REJECTED (Grok): early-return + structured-return (already implemented — described code that doesn't match reality, S53 hallucination pattern); Map-latch + helloSent guard + const-factory (YAGNI); lazy host-response (over-complex, lobby timing kills race).
prime_audit: PRIME-AUDIT delta (Prime Architect, neither Council member had deploy history): wiring HELLO arms FUTURE skew detection ONLY. Every pre-S54 deploy currently in the wild NEVER emits HELLO (it was dormant), so the mismatch UX cannot fire against any present client — first possible trigger is an S54+ peer meeting a future S55+(bumped-protoVersion) peer (both emit HELLO). Therefore this PDR produces ZERO observable behavior change today beyond the happy-path no-op; value is forward-looking insurance completing the S53 half-build. Corollary: the simulated-mismatch integration test (ledger #6) is the ONLY possible pre-ship verification of the path — reinforces the R8 HIGH ADOPT. Runtime-Verifiability (Rule 20): transport.test.ts:1-14 explicitly states 'No Trystero room is constructed' — onMessage->emitProtocolMismatch->latch->drop wiring (transport.ts:351-366) confirmed UNTESTED before this batch. M5 will break the existing assertion at transport.test.ts:167 (Your friend's version is older) — must update in lockstep.

priorities.label: S54 P1 — HELLO emission at peer-join (activate dormant S53 protocol-mismatch system)
priorities.check_method: P1 verified: tsc -b --noEmit clean + full vitest 840/840 GREEN (828->840, +12: +4 buildHello producer tests in protocol.test.ts incl. parseNetMessage wire-round-trip; +8 handleRawMessage receive-path INTEGRATION tests in transport.test.ts). The 8 integration tests close Council R1 #6 (Grok R8 HIGH + Gemini ch.3 CONVERGENT): exercise the full inbound path (JSON parse -> per-peer drop latch -> detectProtocolMismatch sniff -> parseNetMessage -> handler fan-out) WITHOUT a live Trystero room, proving (a) same-version HELLO is a routed no-op firing no mismatch; (b) mismatched HELLO fires onProtocolMismatch(version) once + drops the HELLO; (c) LATCH closure — a subsequent allowlisted INTENT from the latched peer is dropped (the v2-peer-INTENT-bypass desync gap); (d) idempotent once-per-peer; (e) other peers unaffected; (f) malformed JSON -> emitError no-throw; (g) structurally-invalid msg -> rejected, no mismatch. RALPH:PATROL hypotheses all clear: no handler-leak (fresh NetTransport per Host/Join), send() cannot throw at join (connect() sets connected=true synchronously before any async peer-join), handleRawMessage is a behavior-preserving pure refactor (all pre-existing transport tests pass), backpressure negligible (~50B HELLO once per join). Both-sides emit retained (Council #2 OVERRULE of Grok host-only — host-side latch requires the joiner's HELLO). Bundle 498.51->498.73 KB (+0.22 KB; 1.27 KB headroom under 500 KB charter). PRIME-AUDIT caveat: activation arms FUTURE protocol-skew detection only (pre-S54 peers emit no HELLO) — zero observable behavior today beyond happy-path no-op. Commit bb40c90 pushed origin/master; CI E2E run 26639170956 + Deploy 26639170986 triggered.

priorities.label: S54 P2 — CHECK-CARRY-S54 LOW cluster (G3 sanitize / M4 type-guard / M5 neutral UX)
priorities.check_method: P2 verified: tsc -b --noEmit clean + full vitest 842/842 GREEN (840->842, +2: M5 neutral-language test + G3 non-primitive sanitize test; the existing transport.test.ts:167 assertion updated in lockstep per PRIME-AUDIT). G3 — describePeerVersion() collapses a non-null object/array/function to '(object)'/'(array)'/'(function)' (also a minor security plus: bypasses any malicious toString on a crafted peerVersion); primitives render verbatim (number/string/undefined preserved; existing :184-188 tests pass). M4 — isObjectRecord(v): v is Record<string,unknown> type-guard replaces the 'parsed as Record' cast in detectProtocolMismatch; behavior-preserving (arrays still return mismatch:false via the kind-check; all 7 detectProtocolMismatch tests + the :143 array case pass). M5 — neutral 'The other player's version is older' (Council #3 unanimous). Bundle 498.73->498.87 KB (+0.14 KB; 1.13 KB headroom). CHECK Triumvirate: RALPH:PATROL PASS + Gemini-AUDITOR PASS (5/5 Fidelity/Quality/Tool/Completeness, zero findings) + GROK-ANALYST 4 findings ALL adjudicated FALSE-POSITIVE via empirical verification — CRITICAL 'strategyName never used' refuted (used at transport.ts:262, passed at :411); HIGH 'HELLO sent before remote onMessage registered' refuted (onMessage :411 precedes onPeerJoin :413 within synchronous startStrategy, which runs inside connect() before any peer can join — cited mechanism impossible); MED 'isObjectRecord rejects arrays' refuted (isObjectRecord([])===true, behavior preserved); LOW 'primitive rendering uninformative' refuted (intended + tested at :187). Continues the documented S53 Grok-CHECK-hallucination pattern. Council verdict SHIP. Commit f7fc208 pushed origin/master; Deploy SUCCESS. END-OF-SESSION AUDIT (Rule 22) runtime-pass: first E2E run failed on smoke.spec.ts:256 (Sym F territorial hard-block); re-run on identical commit GREEN — Playwright marked it '1 flaky' (failed-then-passed-on-retry, 5 passed 4.0m). Confirms the documented pre-existing Sym F flake recurred (also failed the S53 P1 push when HELLO was NOT sent), NOT an S54 regression — HELLO is a lobby-phase no-op causally disconnected from the test's spark-spawn/drag-place/snapshot-propagation timing. The 5 passing E2E tests include the full 2-peer connect->play->placement flow, so HELLO activation did not regress the happy-path connection. No GitHub issues opened.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S54 #hello-emission-activates-dormant-s53-protocol-mismatch-system — the S53 P1 mismatch latch + UX shipped tested-but-dormant because NO code sent a HELLO. S54 wired the missing producer: buildHello() in protocol.ts + wireHelloOnJoin() registered via transport.onPeerChange('join') on both host (P0/crimson) + joiner (P1/cyan). A 'tested but never invoked' feature is a recurring trap — the producer call site is as load-bearing as the consumer logic; grep the SEND side, not just the parse side, when auditing a wire feature.

S54 #wire-hello-at-join-not-connect — transport.send() broadcasts to CURRENTLY-CONNECTED peers, so a HELLO sent at connect() time (peerCount=0) is silently dropped ('no strategy ready yet'). Correct hook is onPeerChange('join'), which fires once per peerId (deduped across strategies) when the data channel opens. onPeerChange was previously unused API (main.ts polls peerCount() for the connection-lost overlay) so registering it was conflict-free. Lobby-phase timing guarantees the HELLO precedes any snapshot/intent traffic — no readiness-flag/sendQueue needed (Council #1).

S54 #both-sides-hello-required-overrule-grok-host-only — Council Grok argued host-only HELLO suffices ('host rejects on receipt'). OVERRULED: the host-side drop latch can only fire if it RECEIVES the joiner's HELLO; without it, a skewed joiner's semantically-valid INTENT passes the allowlist and desyncs the host — the exact v2-peer-INTENT-bypass gap the S53 latch exists to close. A domain-expert vote built on a false premise is overruled with the artifact (S53 CONVERGENT BLOCKER), not conceded on weight.

S54 #handlerawmessage-receive-seam-makes-latch-testable — extracting the inbound action.onMessage closure into a public handleRawMessage(data,peerId,strategyName) method (pure refactor) made the entire receive path (parse->latch->mismatch-sniff->parse->fan-out) unit-testable WITHOUT a live Trystero room — the file's long-standing 'no room is constructed' limitation. 8 integration tests then proved the dormant latch fires/latches/drops end-to-end. Lesson: when a stateful path is 'untestable without infra', extract a method seam rather than leaving it unverified (Council #6 ADOPT; closed Grok R8 HIGH + Gemini ch.3).

S54 #g3-describe-peer-version-also-blocks-tostring-injection — sanitizing a non-primitive peerVersion to '(object)'/'(array)' (vs String() -> '[object Object]') has a latent security benefit: it never invokes a peer-controlled toString(), so a malicious crafted object can't inject arbitrary text into the lobby diagnostic. A defensive-rendering fix doubled as an input-trust fix.

S54 #grok-check-hallucination-pattern-recurs — GROK-ANALYST CHECK produced 4 findings, ALL false-positives on empirical verification (CRITICAL 'strategyName never used' — it's used at transport.ts:262; HIGH 'HELLO before remote onMessage registered' — onMessage registers before onPeerJoin within synchronous connect(); MED 'isObjectRecord rejects arrays' — it returns true for arrays; LOW intended+tested). Third consecutive session (S53 R1 + S53 CHECK + S54 CHECK) of Grok hallucinating absence-of-usage or inverted code logic. The PRIME-AUDIT/CHECK fact-check rule (verify every cited file/line/claim via Read/Grep before adopting) paid off again — 0 wasted fixes. Consider weighting Grok CHECK findings as hypotheses-to-verify, not findings, until calibration improves.

S54 #prime-audit-hello-arms-future-skew-only — wiring HELLO now produces ZERO observable behavior today: every pre-S54 deploy never emits HELLO, so the mismatch UX can't fire against any current client; first possible trigger is an S54+ peer meeting a future S55+(bumped-protoVersion) peer. The value is forward-looking insurance completing the S53 half-build, not a fix for a present bug. Stating this honestly prevents over-claiming 'users now see mismatch errors'. Neither Council member surfaced it (no deploy-history context) — the Prime Architect's PRIME-AUDIT pass remains the load-bearing reality check.
